metric_name: SAP R/3 on AS/400 general availability Q2 1996
metric_value: Study predicted controlled release Q1 1996, general availability Q2 1996; initially targeting enterprises with >$300M revenue
notes: Roadmap stated in study body per README

metric_name: SAP R/3 on AS/400 availability outcome
metric_value: IBM announced availability of SAP R/3 on IBM AS/400 in 1996 per LinkedIn IBM-SAP 50-year history article. IBM Redbooks published 'Implementing SAP R/3 on OS/400' in 2001, confirming successful platform deployment.
notes: Source: LinkedIn IBM 52-year partnership article (2022); IBM Redbooks SG24-4672 (2001). Prediction confirmed.

metric_name: AS/400 with 64-bit PowerPC AS as compelling enterprise platform
metric_value: Study positioned AS/400 Advanced Series as a long-term enterprise platform due to simplicity, integration, and 64-bit RISC advantages
notes: Core thesis of study

metric_value: AS/400 evolved through iSeries (2000), System i (2006), IBM Power Systems/IBM i (2008-present). As of 2025, 32.1% of install base still on IBM i 7.1+. Platform remained commercially successful for 30+ years confirming Aberdeen's thesis.
notes: Sources: LANSA blog 2025; LinkedIn Legacy IBM i guide 2026. Prediction fully confirmed.

metric_name: SAP R/3 continued growth dominance as ERP platform
metric_value: SAP America near-100% YoY growth; R/3 at 85% of North American revenues; first vendor to $1B; high R&D spend signals continued innovation
notes: Implied by financial data in obs-05 through obs-10

metric_value: SAP became the global ERP leader, dominating the market through 2000s. R/3 evolved to SAP ECC, then S/4HANA (2015). IBM became SAP's largest customer in 1994 and partnership deepened. SAP SE had revenues of ~$35B by 2023.
notes: Source: HFS Research IBM-SAP 52-year partnership article (2024); LinkedIn IBM-SAP 50-year history. Prediction confirmed emphatically.
